title: podman machine start fails with "gvproxy exited unexpectedly with exit code 0"
body:
### Issue Description

When starting a container with provider=applehv, `podman machine start` fails with `gvproxy unixgram socket "/var/folders/82/c0s0s7md2m1c5nzd6s08j1tn09h343/T/podman/gvproxy.sock" not found: stat /var/folders/82/c0s0s7md2m1c5nzd6s08j1tn09h343/T/podman/gvproxy.sock: no such file or directory`. This appears not to be a duplicate of #21288 because `${TMPDIR%%/}/podman/gvproxy.sock"` exists, but for some reason podman claims it doesn't. Also, the problem occurs without rebooting the host first.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. export CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=applehv
2. podman machine init
3. podman --log-level=debug machine start


### Describe the results you received

Error message is similar to #21288:
```
$ podman --log-level=debug machine start
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Using Podman machine with `applehv` virtualization provider 
DEBU[0000] connection refused: http://localhost:8081/vm/state 
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
DEBU[0000] connection refused: http://localhost:8081/vm/state 
DEBU[0000] gvproxy binary being used: /opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.9.0/libexec/podman/gvproxy 
DEBU[0000] [-debug -mtu 1500 -ssh-port 56066 -listen-vfkit unixgram:///var/folders/82/c0s0s7md2m1c5nzd6s08j1tn09h343/T/podman/gvproxy.sock -forward-sock /Users/haiducek/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv/podman.sock -forward-dest /run/user/1083706499/podman/podman.sock -forward-user core -forward-identity /Users/haiducek/.ssh/podman-machine-default -pid-file /var/folders/82/c0s0s7md2m1c5nzd6s08j1tn09h343/T/podman/gvproxy.pid] 
DEBU[0000] gvproxy unixgram socket "/var/folders/82/c0s0s7md2m1c5nzd6s08j1tn09h343/T/podman/gvproxy.sock" not found: stat /var/folders/82/c0s0s7md2m1c5nzd6s08j1tn09h343/T/podman/gvproxy.sock: no such file or directory 
Error: gvproxy exited unexpectedly with exit code 0
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines                        
```

But unlike #21288, /var/folders/82/c0s0s7md2m1c5nzd6s08j1tn09h343/T/podman exists:
```
$ ls /var/folders/82/c0s0s7md2m1c5nzd6s08j1tn09h343/T/podman
podman-machine-default_ready.sock
```

### Describe the results you expected

podman machine start runs successfully

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

OS: darwin/arm64
provider: applehv
version: 4.9.0

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:56066: connect: connection refused
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Host is MacOS Monterey (12.6.1)

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: The error is `gvproxy exited unexpectedly with exit code 0`

This can be reproduced with Podman Desktop pre-release 1.7.0 (podman 4.9.0) when following the installation instructions for installing from .dmg here (without any brew): https://gist.github.com/jfrantzius/0996e224cbe81d885553ece6f8abf99a
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
me too with podman 5.0 release on apple m2 with applehv
Closing as there is no response and there have been fixes in 5.X since.

Not sure if anyone else is facing the same issue, I had to install below package to solve the issue -

`sudo dnf install podman-gvproxy`
